what_it_does: Defines a static HTML presentation page showing three mobile app state mockups: failed save, offline mode, and a first-run permission ask, with inline page-specific styles and runtime-inserted icons.
observations: The page title is "App — when it goes wrong (v1)" and the visible heading pairs "Candor, Then Resolution" with "When It Goes Wrong, Say It Plainly".
Three phone mockups are arranged inside a shared presentation frame, each with a captioned role below it.
The first phone shows a failed-save state with an exclamation mark, the message "The Entry Didn't Bind", and actions to bind again or keep working.
The second phone shows an offline state with "— offline" in the status bar, a "12h" circular mark, and copy saying work holds until the tide returns.
The third phone shows a first-run ask with two request cards: camera access for photographs and a single notification when the kiln peaks.
Inline CSS defines the centered state layout, circular marks, and ask cards while relying on shared CSS files for the broader piece and phone scaffolding.
A short script inserts two icons through DNA.org.icon into empty spans with ids a1 and a2.
standouts: Failure, offline, and permission states are framed as recoverable rather than blocking.
The warning visuals are compact circular marks paired with short explanatory copy.
Permission copy names the need and scope inside separate ask cards.
conventions: Uses shared design tokens through CSS custom properties for color, spacing, type, and radius.
Uses existing phone-frame class conventions such as view, title, phones, unit, phone, sbar, p-hd, p-act, p-ghost, and role.
Keeps page-specific CSS inline in the document head.
Uses absolute positioning for the central state content and bottom ghost buttons.
Uses the shared DNA.org.icon runtime helper for icons instead of embedding icon markup directly.
concerns: Several layout details are placed in inline style attributes, which makes spacing and positioning less centralized within the file.
The icon script assumes /surface/runtime/organisms.js loads successfully and exposes DNA.org.icon before the script runs.
The icon spans have no fallback content if the runtime icon insertion fails.
notes: A static app-state study focused on plain recovery messaging for failed save, offline work, and permission requests.
questions: Does "12h" represent an actual offline duration, a sync window, or only a state label?
Is the "plan" icon intended to represent camera access, or is a camera-specific icon expected elsewhere?
Is "Nothing leaves the workshop" meant as a literal privacy guarantee for the app?
Are the bottom ghost buttons meant to share one positioning pattern across all phone states?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file presents app states for failure, offline operation, and permission asking with plain recovery-oriented copy.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to demonstrate an app voice that states problems plainly, reassures the user that work is preserved, and gives a direct next action without making the state visually loud.
novelty: Generic app states are expressed through ledger and workshop language such as bind, bench, tide, wire, seams, workshop, and kiln.
contribution: This file contributes recoverable-state patterns: compact error and offline markers, local-preservation reassurance, direct recovery actions, and permission requests tied to concrete app uses.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML mockup page showing three mobile app states for failed saving, offline work, and permission asking in a ledger/workshop-themed interface.
intention_for_embedding: This was for exploring how an app can communicate problems and access requests plainly while keeping the user oriented and able to continue.
